regime_read: ES & NQ moving together — broad agreement, more DURABLE directional signal. Trust the direction more.
narrative:
Macro backdrop —
[2026-07-14] US economic state (FRED):
  Growth: real GDP 2.1% (q/q ann)
  Inflation: CPI 3.67% YoY | core CPI 2.26% | core PCE 2.89% (Fed target 2%)
  Labor: unemployment 4.2%
  Rates: fed funds 3.63% | 10Y 4.56% | 10Y-2Y 0.36% (NORMAL)
  Consumer: UMich sentiment 44.8 | VIX 15.03

---
PRE-US-OPEN BRIEFING — ES/NQ (as of 2026-07-14 12:15 UTC)

1) RECENT MOVE-BY-MOVE READ
The overnight/prior-session tape is being dominated by geopolitics, not earnings or macro data. Multiple headlines flag US-Iran escalation and tightening of the Strait of Hormuz — a classic risk-off / energy-shock cocktail that pressures equity futures while lifting crude. That matches the tape: ES sits at 7549, below its session VWAP (7559) and below prior-day high (7615.25), printing a modest daily loss (-0.19%). ES traded down toward and is now hovering just above its prior-day low (7547.25) and prior-week low (7468.5) is still well beneath — so this is a controlled pullback, not a breakdown. NQ tells a slightly different story: it's actually GREEN on the day (+0.42%) at 29599, but has been rejected from prior-day high (30041) and is sitting essentially ON its VWAP (29588). The tech-flavored headline (RTX / semis chatter) is noise, not a driver. Net read: geopolitics is the catalyst pushing ES softer while NQ shows relative resilience — a mild bid divergence at the index level, but both remain firmly above their 200-day SMAs (ES +7.8%, NQ +12.2%), so the medium-term uptrend is intact and this is a headline-driven wobble inside a bull structure.

2) VOLUME-PROFILE READ (WHERE volume traded — NOT order-flow delta)
Caveat first: this is volume-at-price over 5 days, not buy/sell aggression. No true delta here. For NQ, POC = 29870.9 with a value area of 29480.69–30056.71. Price at 29599 is sitting in the LOWER third of the value area, below the POC — meaning we've traded down into value but not out of it. The accumulation shelf / high-volume nodes cluster up around 29833–29945; that overhead is where sellers/supply live and where any bounce likely stalls. For ES, POC = 7587.35, value area 7551.22–7604.2; ES at 7549 is right at/just below value-area-low — the marginal spot where acceptance below VAL would signal a shift toward the lower distribution. LINE IN THE SAND: NQ value-area-low ~29480 is the near-term pivot; holding above it keeps price in balance, losing it opens rotation toward the 29322 range low. ES VAL ~7551 is the mirror level to watch.

3) NQ/ES LEAD-LAG REGIME
Contemporaneous correlation is high at 0.905 and the lead-lag matrix peaks cleanly at lag 0 (0.882) with no meaningful predictive lead in either direction (best off-zero |corr| is just 0.085 — noise). System classifies this as BREADTH: ES and NQ moving together, which historically is the more DURABLE, trust-the-direction regime. The catch: today they're mildly diverging (ES red, NQ green; divergence flag = true, 1d spread +0.60%). So we have a durable-correlation regime but a fresh short-term spread wobble. That combination says: trust direction MORE when it resolves, but right now direction hasn't resolved — the two indices disagree on the day. That argues for patience, not size. (Reminder: regime is an INPUT, not an edge; ES/NQ here is research-only — no execution path.)

4) EVENT-CALENDAR AWARENESS
FOMC is 15 days out (2026-07-29), PCE ~17 days (7-31, estimate), ISM Manufacturing ~20 days (estimate). None are imminent enough to force size reduction today, but the FOMC window begins to matter next week — plan to trim size / widen stops as we approach. The more pressing risk is EVENT-LIKE geopolitics: Hormuz/Iran headlines can gap the tape at any hour regardless of the calendar, which is a size-down argument on its own for overnight/held exposure.

5) SYNTHESIS — META-REGIME + NARROWED BIAS
Meta-regime: durable BREADTH backdrop, bull structure intact (both above 200SMA), but a headline-driven risk-off pull and an unresolved ES/NQ intraday divergence. News is CLEAR in theme (geopolitics = risk-off pressure) but the lead-lag is clean-but-diverging today, and price is mid-value, not at an edge. Per my own rule, that's a MIXED setup — I do NOT get high confidence. I'm also humble on the scorecard: n=12, still calibrating, regime accuracy weak (0.286), so treat this as intelligence only.

BIAS: NEUTRAL-to-slightly-cautious, ~42% confidence. Inside value, I don't chase. The actionable framing: NQ holding above ~29480 (value-area-low) keeps a mild long-the-dip lean alive with an upside magnet toward POC 29870; acceptance below ~29480 flips the read toward rotation-lower into 29322. INVALIDATION for any long lean: below 29386.5 (prior-day low) — a break there confirms the geopolitical risk-off is winning and NQ has lost its intraday resilience. No high-conviction directional trade here; wait for value-edge resolution or a fresh, cleaner catalyst.
